name: Nagaland (Nāgāland)
summary: Nagaland is a North-eastern Indian Himalayan-foothills mixed common-law/Naga-customary-law constituent state of the Indian Republic — structurally distinctive globally as the only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-A special-status framework explicitly protecting Naga customary law (Article 371-A(1)(a)(i)-(iv) provides that no Act of Parliament shall apply to Nagaland in respect of (i) religious or social practices of the Nagas, (ii) Naga customary law and procedure, (iii) administration of civil and criminal justice involving decisions according to Naga customary law, and (iv) ownership and transfer of land and resources, unless the Nagaland Legislative Assembly so decides by a resolution), as the central jurisdiction of the longest-running modern Indian-state-level armed insurgency (Naga National Movement 1947-present, with various Naga insurgent groups including NSCN-IM, NSCN-K, NNC operating from 1947 with sustained Indian Government peace talks since 1997 'Framework Agreement' processes), and as the only Indian state with explicit pre-constitutional Article 371-A protection guaranteeing that Indian Parliament Acts do not automatically apply in matters of Naga customary law. Nagaland was formed as the 16th state of India on 1 December 1963 under the State of Nagaland Act 1962 — first Indian state created from a Union Territory framework. Nagaland comprises ~16,579 km² with a population of ~2 million. Family-law framework operates under a dual federal Indian common-law + Naga customary-law framework with Naga customary law applied via Naga Customary Courts (Village/Range/Subordinate-District-District Customary Courts) operating under tribal Customary Law per Article 371-A. Parental authority and child custody operate under Naga customary law for Naga persons subject to Indian general framework as modified by Article 371-A. The Gauhati High Court (Kohima Bench) is the apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Nagaland; final appellate jurisdiction lies with the Supreme Court of India. Nagaland is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label. India is a Hague Convention 1980 signatory but not a party — Nagaland is governed by the Indian non-ratification status.

statutory_framework.citation: Constitution of India Article 371-A (Nagaland Special Provisions)
statutory_framework.title: Constitution of India Article 371-A
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Article inserted by Constitution (Thirteenth Amendment) Act 1962 establishing special provisions for Nagaland — explicitly protecting Naga religious/social practices, Naga customary law and procedure, administration of justice involving Naga customary law, and ownership/transfer of Naga lands and resources.

statutory_framework.title: State of Nagaland Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Federal Act of 1962 establishing the State of Nagaland effective 1 December 1963 — first Indian state created from Union Territory framework.

statutory_framework.title: Constitution 13th Amendment Act
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Constitutional Amendment Act of 1962 inserting Article 371-A providing Naga customary law constitutional protection.

statutory_framework.title: Naga Sixteen-Point Agreement
statutory_framework.relevance: Indian Government - Naga People's Convention agreement of 27 July 1960 establishing framework for Nagaland statehood and Article 371-A protections.

apex_courts.name: Gauhati High Court (Kohima Bench)
apex_courts.role: Apex domestic appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Nagaland.

apex_courts.name: Supreme Court of India
apex_courts.role: Final appellate court for civil and criminal matters from Nagaland.

apex_courts.name: Naga Customary Courts (Village/Range/Subordinate-District-District)
apex_courts.role: Naga customary courts operating under tribal Customary Law per Article 371-A.

professional_regulators.name: Indian Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI)
professional_regulators.role: Indian Federal professional regulatory framework applicable in Nagaland.

anonymisation_convention: Nagaland family-court decisions are anonymised per Indian court practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Naga Hills District + British colonial framework
key_developments.description: Naga Hills District established 1881 within Assam Province under British colonial framework — pivotal pre-modern Naga-British colonial framework foundation + foundation for subsequent 1947 Naga National Council independence declaration framework + Naga customary-law framework preserved within British colonial framework.

key_developments.title: Naga National Council Declaration of Independence
key_developments.description: Naga National Council declared Naga independence on 14 August 1947 (one day before Indian independence) under A.Z. Phizo's leadership — beginning of Naga National Movement + structurally distinctive globally longest-running modern Indian-state-level armed insurgency framework foundation 1947-present.

key_developments.title: Naga Federal Government formed
key_developments.description: Naga Federal Government formed in 1956 by Naga National Council — establishing Naga underground government framework + significant context for subsequent 1960 Sixteen-Point Agreement and 1963 Nagaland statehood framework + foundation for subsequent NSCN splits 1980+.

key_developments.title: Sixteen-Point Agreement (Naga People's Convention)
key_developments.description: Indian Government - Naga People's Convention agreement of 27 July 1960 establishing framework for Nagaland statehood and Article 371-A protections — pivotal modern Naga-Indian government negotiated-settlement framework + foundation for subsequent Constitutional 13th Amendment + State of Nagaland Act 1962 framework.

key_developments.title: Constitution 13th Amendment + Article 371-A
key_developments.description: Indian Constitution (Thirteenth Amendment) Act 1962 inserting Article 371-A providing Naga customary law constitutional protection — structurally distinctive globally only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-A special-status framework explicitly protecting Naga customary law + Article 371-A pre-constitutional protection where Indian Parliament Acts do not automatically apply in matters of Naga customary law.

key_developments.title: Nagaland State established
key_developments.description: Nagaland formed as 16th state of India on 1 December 1963 under the State of Nagaland Act 1962 — structurally distinctive globally first Indian state created from Union Territory framework + pivotal modern Indian-state foundation framework + foundation for subsequent Naga Customary Courts framework + 1975 Shillong Accord + 1997 NSCN-IM ceasefire.

key_developments.title: NSCN formation + Naga insurgency reconfiguration
key_developments.description: National Socialist Council of Nagaland (NSCN) formed 31 January 1980 by Isak Chishi Swu + Thuingaleng Muivah + S.S. Khaplang — pivotal reconfiguration of Naga insurgency framework following 1975 Shillong Accord controversy + subsequent 1988 NSCN split into NSCN-IM (Muivah-Swu) + NSCN-K (Khaplang) + continuing fragmentation framework.

key_developments.title: NSCN-IM ceasefire + Indian Government peace talks
key_developments.description: NSCN-IM ceasefire agreement with Indian Government 1 August 1997 — beginning of sustained Indian Government peace talks framework + ~27-year continuous ceasefire framework + foundation for subsequent 2015 Framework Agreement + ongoing peace-process framework.

key_developments.title: Framework Agreement
key_developments.description: Indian Government - NSCN-IM Framework Agreement of 3 August 2015 signed by Indian Government interlocutor R.N. Ravi + Thuingaleng Muivah — comprehensive framework for Naga political resolution including Naga sovereignty within Indian Constitution framework + implementation ongoing context preceding 2024 framework continuation.

key_developments.title: Neiphiu Rio NDPP Government + AFSPA-partial-rollback + Eastern-Nagaland-People-Organisation framework
key_developments.description: Neiphiu Rio (NDPP-BJP) Chief Minister framework continuing through 2023 following March 2023 Assembly elections with AFSPA partial rollback from 8 districts April 2023 substantively significant institutional context affecting Naga family-law-implementation framework. Eastern Nagaland People's Organisation (ENPO) institutional positions on separate Frontier Nagaland Territory framework + Naga Customary Courts (Village/Range/Subordinate-District-District) institutional positions addressing PA-related issues within Article 371-A framework. Substantively significant North-eastern Indian Himalayan-foothills institutional jurisprudential development.

key_developments.title: Continuing Naga peace process + Article 371-A framework
key_developments.description: Continuing Naga peace process 2024 — continuing 1997 NSCN-IM ceasefire + continuing 2015 Framework Agreement implementation negotiations + ongoing AFSPA (Armed Forces Special Powers Act 1958) Nagaland extension framework + 2021 Mon district Oting incident context + continuing Article 371-A constitutional framework + 77 years since 1947 Naga National Council Declaration of Independence.

structural_findings: Nagaland operates a mixed federal Indian common-law + Naga customary-law framework — places Nagaland in the North-eastern Indian Indigenous-tribal-customary-law-protected cluster.
Only Indian state operating constitutional Article 371-A special-status framework explicitly protecting Naga customary law is structurally distinctive globally.

Article 371-A pre-constitutional protection framework where Indian Parliament Acts do not automatically apply in matters of Naga customary law is structurally distinctive globally — Naga customary law has supra-Federal-Act status in Naga customary-law matters.
Central jurisdiction of longest-running modern Indian-state-level armed insurgency (Naga National Movement 1947-present) is structurally distinctive globally.
First Indian state created from Union Territory framework (1962-1963) is structurally distinctive within Indian state-formation cluster.

Naga Customary Courts (Village/Range/Subordinate-District-District) framework operating under Article 371-A constitutional protection is structurally distinctive globally — modern multi-tier indigenous-customary-court framework.
Sixteen-Point Agreement 1960 + 2015 Framework Agreement Indian Government peace-talks framework is structurally distinctive.
editorial_notes: Nagaland jurisdiction sidecar v1.1 — mixed federal Indian common-law/Naga customary-law North-eastern Indian Himalayan-foothills Indian-state (1881 Naga Hills District British colonial framework + 1947 Naga National Council Declaration of Independence + 1956 Naga Federal Government + 1960 Sixteen-Point Agreement + 1962 Constitution 13th Amendment Article 371-A + 1963 Nagaland State established + 1980 NSCN formation + 1997 NSCN-IM ceasefire + 2015 Framework Agreement + 2024 continuing Naga peace process with AFSPA framework). Only Indian state operating Article 371-A constitutional Naga-customary-law protection globally + Article 371-A pre-constitutional protection where Indian Parliament Acts do not automatically apply in matters of Naga customary law + central jurisdiction of longest-running modern Indian-state-level armed insurgency (Naga National Movement 1947-present) + first Indian state created from Union Territory framework + modern multi-tier indigenous-customary-court framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.

Joins North-eastern-Indian + Himalayan-foothills + mixed Indian-common-law/Naga-customary-law + Indian-state cluster + Article-371-A-Naga-customary-law-constitutional-protection-globally-distinctive + Article-371-A-pre-constitutional-protection-framework + Naga-National-Movement-1947-present-longest-Indian-state-level-armed-insurgency + Sixteen-Point-Agreement-1960 + 2015-Framework-Agreement + Naga-Customary-Courts-multi-tier-framework + first-Indian-state-from-Union-Territory + Indian-non-Hague-1980 clusters within the corpus.
